channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran and Oman work on Strait of Hormuz shipping guidelines
item.description: More than 40 nations are currently engaged in discussions aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil supplies. Iran and Oman are leading efforts to draft a formal tran...

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II Mission Begins First Crewed Lunar Flyby in Over Five Decades
item.description: NASA has initiated a new era of human space exploration with the launch of Artemis II, the first crewed mission to orbit the Moon in more than 50 years. The mission, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, ...

item.title: Race Across the World Series 6: A Heartfelt Journey Across Eight Countries
item.description: The sixth series of 'Race Across the World' returns with its trademark blend of adventure, human connection, and cultural exploration. Five duos are challenged to traverse 12,000 kilometres from Paler...

item.title: Study shows bumblebees can learn and recognise abstract rhythms
item.description: Recent research from Southern Medical University and Macquarie University has revealed that bumblebees possess the remarkable ability to perceive and learn rhythms, despite having brains no bigger tha...

item.title: Private Schools in Islamabad Shift to Four-Day Academic Week Amid Fuel Conservation Measures
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Private Educational Institutions Regulatory Authority, under the Ministry of Federal Education, has directed all private schools in Islamabad to implement a four-day workweek for on-cam...

item.title: Gilas Pilipinas 3x3 Advances to FIBA Asia Cup Main Draw After Narrow Win Over Thailand
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Gilas Pilipinas 3×3 delivered an impressive performance against the reigning Southeast Asian Games champions Thailand, winning 17-15 to secure a spot in the main draw of the FIBA...

item.title: Met Office issues warnings as Storm Dave brings high winds and snow to northern UK over Easter
item.description: Storm Dave, the fourth named storm of the year, is expected to affect parts of the UK this Easter weekend with severe winds and possible snow. The Met Office has issued yellow warnings for wind across...

item.title: Defence Minister Confirms Fuel Supplies Stable as Navy Escorts Tankers from Hormuz
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh has reassured the nation that there is no shortage of fuel or gas in India despite the ongoing West Asia conflict. This statement comes alongside confirmation that the I...

item.title: JEE Main 2026 Starts in Saudi Arabia with Full Embassy Support
item.description: The Joint Entrance Examination (JEE-Main) 2026 has officially started in Saudi Arabia, as confirmed by the Indian Embassy in Riyadh. Conducted by the National Testing Agency (NTA), JEE-Main is a cruci...

item.title: Kogi State Records Significant Drop in Malaria Cases Between 2021 and 2025
item.description: Kogi State don show serious progress for health sector as malaria cases don reduce sharply from 16 percent in 2021 to 8.9 percent in 2025 according to the latest National Malaria Indicator Survey. The...

item.title: Investigation reveals serious misconduct at Hull funeral home
item.description: Robert Bush, a funeral director in Hull, has admitted to 30 counts of preventing lawful burials and multiple counts of defrauding families. Over several years, his funeral home, Legacy Independent Fun...

item.title: Derek Chisora attends press conference with Nigel Farage ahead of final fight
item.description: British heavyweight boxer Derek Chisora made a striking entrance at a London press conference for his forthcoming fight against Deontay Wilder at the O2 Arena. Accompanied by Reform UK leader Nigel Fa...

item.title: BBC terminates Scott Mills' contract following new information about historic police investigation
item.description: The BBC has terminated the contract of radio host Scott Mills following revelations about a historic police investigation. Mills, 53, who presented the Radio 2 breakfast show, was dismissed after it e...

item.title: Iran launches retaliatory strikes on UAE, Bahrain and Israel after US-Israeli attacks
item.description: On Thursday, Iran carry out fresh attacks wey dem claim target UAE, Bahrain and Israel. This na reaction to strikes wey US and Israeli forces don launch on Iran's two biggest steel plants earlier. Acc...

item.title: Nick Candy sells historic Chelsea property in London for record £270 million
item.description: British entrepreneur Nick Candy has sold his family residence in London’s prestigious Chelsea district for over £270 million ($517 million), marking what is believed to be the largest single house sal...

item.title: Philippine Government Standardizes Filipino Names for All Features in Kalayaan Island Group
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has mandated that all 131 geographical features in the Kalayaan Island Group (KIG) of the West Philippine Sea be officially named using Filipino terms. Through Executive...

item.title: Universities in Pakistan to adopt four-day workweek amid fuel crisis
item.description: The Higher Education Commission (HEC) of Pakistan has officially introduced a four-day workweek for all universities in the country as part of the federal government's austerity measures aimed at cons...

item.title: Archaeological Study Shows Native Americans Used Dice 12,000 Years Ago
item.description: A new study published in American Antiquity reveals that Native American hunter-gatherers were making and using dice over 12,000 years ago, long before similar tools appeared in the Old World. Researc...

item.title: Nova Scotia man faces charges for producing and possessing child sexual abuse material
item.description: Authorities in Nova Scotia have charged a man from Newellton with offences related to child sexual abuse material. The charges include both making and possessing such content, highlighting ongoing law...

item.title: Uniqlo PH introduces t-shirt collection inspired by popular theater shows
item.description: Uniqlo Philippines is set to launch a new t-shirt collection dedicated to theater enthusiasts, featuring designs inspired by iconic musicals such as 'Wicked,' 'Les Miserables,' 'Cats,' and 'Phantom of...
